subject: Account Notification! (mess_id: ZT67518468)
Please Update Your Account Access
body: New Page 1 Dear customers: Wells Fargo is constantly working to increase security for all Online Banking users. To ensure the integrity of our online payment system, we periodically review accounts. Your account might be place on restricted status. Restricted accounts continue to receive payments, but they are limited in their ability to send or withdraw funds. To lift up this restriction, you need to login into your account (with your username or SSN and your password), then you have to complete our verification process. You must confirm your credit card details and your billing information as well. All restricted accounts have their billing information unconfirmed, meaning that you may no longer send money from your account until you have updated your billing information on file. To initiate the billing update confirmation process, please follow the link bellow and fill in the necessary fields: Thank you, Wells Fargo - Online Banking About Wells Fargo | Employment | Report Email Fraud | Privacy, Security & Legal | Home © 1995 - 2005 Wells Fargo. All rights reserved.

subject: API Monitoring - False Positive Alert?
body:
Hi again,

We've been receiving some alerts from the API Monitoring platform about unusually high latency on one of our endpoints. However, after investigating the logs and our own monitoring tools, we can't find any evidence of an actual issue.

Is it possible this is a false positive? How can we troubleshoot this further?

Thanks,
Isiah

subject: Suggestion for Cloud Management Console - IAM Role Integration
body:
Hi Aetheros Support Team,

I'd like to suggest an improvement for the Cloud Management Console. It would be incredibly helpful if we could directly assign IAM roles to resources within the console interface.

Currently, we have to switch between the console and the IAM service to manage these roles. This integration would streamline our workflow and reduce the potential for errors.

Thank you for considering this suggestion.

Sincerely,
Isabella Miller

body: Little Moon performs “Wonder Eye,” plus new songs View this email online “I love being here,” Emma Hardyman says in Little Moon’s Tiny Desk concert. “I say that with the utmost relief!” After watching nearly 6,000 Contest entries, we at NPR were so thrilled to finally welcome Emma and the band (including a big, beautiful harp) to the Tiny Desk last week. Little Moon put on an exquisite performance packed with emotion and creativity, playing its Contest-winning song, “Wonder Eye,” plus two unreleased tracks (written for a new album).Bob Boilen shared a bit about the day here, noting that he and Bobby Carter “were in awe hearing singer Emma Hardyman’s four-octave voice warming up behind the Desk.” Bob explained how challenging it can be for artists to get the balance and sound levels correct at the Desk without monitors or vocal amplification. But Little Moon’s band members “all pretty much nailed it,” he said.Watch Little Moon's Tiny DeskIt was so special to witness the band’s incredible musicianship, quirky, sweet banter and playful nature behind the Desk. Emma shared her inspiration behind the second song in the set, “We Fall In Our Sleep,” which is about “crazy dreams.” And she shared that the last song, “Kind, Kind Home,” is about “feeling like you don’t have a home … and discovering that maybe if you don’t have a home or feel a little out of place, you can be a kind, kind home.” We’re so glad that Little Moon has found a place in our Tiny home. And we hope the Contest has helped all of you in this community find a place of belonging, too.The 2023 Tiny Desk Contest is presented by Capital One and supported by Guayakí Yerba Mate. Newsletter continues after sponsor message We were so excited to meet our winner in real life and hear them perform — and now it's your turn. You can still get tickets to see Little Moon, plus a handful of other standout artists, at the Tiny Desk Contest On The Road tour this summer at NPRPresents.org.For now, please join us in congratulating Little Moon! We know this Tiny Desk concert is just the beginning for this band.From the Desk, NPR’s Tiny Desk Contest team Listen to your local NPR station. Visit NPR.org to hear live radio from KUNC (edit station). What do you think of today's email? We'd love to hear your thoughts, questions and feedback: tinydeskcontest@npr.orgInterested in connecting with music-lovers and music-makers across the Contest community? Join our Tiny Desk Contest Community Facebook Group!Need a new playlist? Follow NPR Music on Spotify and Apple Music!Looking for more great content? Check out all of our newsletter offerings — including Music, Books, Pop Culture and more! You received this message because you're subscribed to Tiny Desk Contest emails. subject: How new rules leave Wheel-Trans users worse off
body: First Up Plus, mayoral candidates debate arts and housing, and the impact of judicial vacancies ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ First Up By Manuela Vega Good morning. Here’s the latest on TTC accessibility, mayoral debates and judicial vacancies in Ontario courts. DON’T MISS Giovanni Capriotti for The Star transit Wheel-Trans users say they’re being forced to “prove” their disability under new rules For the cost of a subway ride, Wheel-Trans users can get door-to-door taxi service. But under new rules the TTC says are based on the Accessibility for Ontarians with Disabilities Act, users who signed up before 2017 are being asked to re-register so their eligibility can be assessed. While the TTC says the changes help ensure equity, some disabled people say they’re facing extra hurdles to “prove” their disabilities and being left worse off in some cases — without the service they need to get around safely and reliably. Lex Harvey lays out why the TTC has implemented the changes and how one advocate sees the new policy as a “misuse” of an act meant to break down barriers. More: “It’s just such a backwards model to have the disabled person jumping through hoops in order to reduce the burden that it costs the TTC to run Wheel-Trans,” said user Maayan Ziv, who has muscular dystrophy. Word from the TTC: “In the application, we are not asking them to prove their disability but rather we are ensuring that we (are) compliant with AODA legislation by confirming their barriers to using the conventional transit system,” a TTC spokesperson said. Steve Russell/The Star race for city hall Key take-aways from Toronto mayoral debates on housing and the arts Wednesday saw Toronto’s top mayoral candidates participate in three debates over five hours; They weighed in on the future of the city’s arts and culture sector, housing policy and taxes. Each made the case for themselves as the mayor that Scarborough deserves, discussing transit, housing, affordability and safety. Joshua Chong, David Rider and Alyshah Hasham report on the major takeaways. The arts: The candidates debating were Ana Bailao, Brad Bradford, Olivia Chow, Mitzie Hunter, Josh Matlow and Mark Saunders. Saunders was the only candidate missing from the arts debate despite being invited. Housing: Chow, Matlow and Hunter all spoke of involving the city and other governments in building new affordable homes. Saunders countered by saying he wouldn’t live in a government-built home and added “builders build.” More: Bailão, the city’s longtime housing advocate, highlighted her work stopping the sale of TCHC homes and establishing the Housing Now program. But Chow, noting no projects have broken ground, called it “Housing Never.” Steve Russell/The Star courts As courts face judicial vacancies, Ontarians are waiting years for civil hearings Federal Justice Minister David Lametti is again facing criticism for delays in appointing judges. There are dozens of empty positions in Ontario’s Superior Court of Justice — a phenomenon that takes a toll on the civil side that deals with lawsuits, including personal injury, medical malpractice and wrongful dismissal. Court priority going to family matters and criminal cases has also added to the delays, with no constitutional guarantee that civil cases will be dealt with in a timely manner, Jacques Gallant reports. As a result, litigants are being made to wait years and are losing trust in the system. Two people voice their frustrations and describe how the wait “puts your whole life on hold.” By the numbers: In federally appointed courts across Canada, there are now almost 90 judicial vacancies. Of those, 24 are in Ontario’s Superior Court. Go deeper: “It makes you relive the same thing over and over again,” said Jennifer Stride of her delayed case. “When you think you’ve dealt with it, suppressed it, put it where you want it to be — pow, punch in the face, here we go again.” Need a trusty guide through the housing crunch? Living Here, the Star’s free newsletter, can help. Each week, it’ll bring you the analysis and expert advice you need to navigate renting, buying, owning or just getting by in and around Toronto — plus more of the Star’s essential real estate coverage. Sign up for free here. WHAT ELSE A tale of two neighbourhoods: What Chinatown and Kensington Market’s recent past tells us about a changing Toronto. Doug Ford says Bonnie Crombie seeking Liberal leadership while being mayor is a “slap in the face to the residents.” Almost 200 doctors say Alberta health care is in crisis. Here’s why they’re worried. Bonnie Crombie is a game changer. Here’s why Doug Ford is worried about her. A report warns Toronto’s glut of empty office towers could last two more decades. Here’s what some say should be done with them. A private Caribbean island, a French castle — and what else costs a fraction of what you’d spend to own a home in Toronto. A woman who killed a Toronto lawyer with a truck has been sentenced to life in prison. A North York man has been arrested for a Facebook Marketplace rental scam. A trillion-dollar payday is in the making as Ontario’s EV supply chain takes shape at a remarkable pace. Unregulated opioids cause extremely high mortality rates. Here’s why the benefits of a safe supply outweigh the risks. Mattea Roach is advancing to the final round for the “Jeopardy! Masters” tournament. She paid this emotional tribute to her late father. Janet Jackson kept Toronto on its feet with a visual and aural bombardment, Nick Krewen writes. POV Canadian Press/Sean Kilpatrick Justin Trudeau risks paying a heavy price for letting David Johnston put principles over politics, Althia Raj writes. CLOSE-UP Associated Press LOS ANGELES: Tina Turner performs her hit song “What's Love Got to Do With It” on Sept. 2, 1984. Turner, the unstoppable singer and stage performer, died at her home in Switzerland Tuesday after a long illness, according to her manager. She was 83. Thank you for reading. You can reach me and the First Up team at firstup@thestar.ca. I’ll see you back here tomorrow. subject: Urgent: Critical Issue with IAM Service - User Roles Not Applying
body: We are experiencing a critical issue with the Aetheros IAM service. User roles that we configure are not being applied correctly, leading to unauthorized access to sensitive data. This is a major security breach for us. We need this resolved ASAP. Please advise on immediate steps to mitigate this issue. We have already tried revoking and re-assigning roles, but the issue persists.

subject: Concern about Hospital Systems
body: A potential data breach has been detected in our hospital systems, impacting several devices. This may have occurred due to insufficient security protocols and software vulnerabilities. Initial steps, such as resetting passwords and running security scans, have been taken, but the issue persists. Urgent action is required to prevent potential harm. We would greatly appreciate it if you could address this matter urgently and provide a solution to prevent future breaches.

subject: Support for the Investment Optimization Tool
body:
Dear Customer Support,
We are encountering problems with the investment optimization tool. It is not generating accurate predictions, which might be due to outdated data in Magento.
We have already tried updating the data feeds and restarting the analytics service, but the issue still persists.
We would appreciate your assistance in resolving this issue. Please provide guidance on how to update the data and troubleshoot the problem.
If needed, please let us know if you require any additional information from us.
Thank you for your time.

subject: Re: [opensuse] Intel 3945 Wireless Problem opensuse 10.3 - RESOLVED!
body: Joe Sloan wrote: > Chuck Stuettgen wrote: > >> The fix is to in YAST, select Configure on the Wireless Card. Select the >> Hardware Tab, then change the Kernel Module in the Module Name Drop down >> list from the ipw3945 to the iwl3945. Save the settings and exit out of >> YAST. Reboot the laptop. >> >> If the iwl3945 kernel module is not in the list install it first then >> reboot and change. > > > Ack, that is a good fix - except for the reboots; absolutely no need for > any of that, just rmmod the ipw3945 and you're good to go. Here's something I just found and points to where the "real" problem with hidden sites is, when for awhile I assumed it was KNetworkmanager....so did others. For almost 2 weeks, I've been using the ipw3945 driver and the standard ifup method to connect. I just changed from that driver to the iwl3945 driver, stopped the ipw driver and used Yast to setup using the iwl driver with KNetworkmanager.....just to see what happend. I've NOT been able to see hidden sites, including my own at home, with KNetworkmanager.......now I can. So, it would appear that this problem that plagues some others with different chipsets have a problem with the driver, NOT KNetworkmanager. Thanks to Chuck Stuettgen for pointing this out!! Fred -- A: Because it messes up the order in which people normally read text. Q: Why is top-posting such a bad thing?

body: Gentile Cliente, nell'ambito di un progetto di verifica dei data anagrafici forniti durante la sottoscrizione dei servizi di Posteitaliane e stata riscontrata una incongruenza relativa ai dati anagrafici in oggetto da Lei forniti all momento della sottoscrizione contrattuale. L'inserimento dei dati alterati puo costituire motivo di interruzione del servizio secondo gli art. 135 e 137/c da Lei accettati al momento della sottoscrizione, oltre a costituire reato penalmente perseguibile secondo il C.P.P ar.415 del 2001 relativo alla legge contro il riciclaggio e la transparenza dei dati forniti in auto certificazione. Per ovviare al problema e necessaria la verifica e l'aggiornamento dei dati relativi all'anaagrafica dell'Intestatario dei servizi Postali. Effetuare l'aggiornamento dei dati cliccando sul seguente collegamento sicuro: Cordiali Saluti.

subject: My reflections of the Outfoxed podcast with Adam Goldfarb
body:
How do you get a 20% positive reply rate from cold email in 2026?

The unobvious answer is: you stop acting like a cold emailer.

In Outfoxed episode 5, we spoke with Adam Goldfarb about what effective cold email looks like in 2026. I recently published a breakdown explaining how to apply his approach in practical experiments to improve reply rates.

In the blog, you’ll learn:
- How to test whether your email feels like spam using the "Nuisance Test"
- Why 80% of your time should go into research and 20% into writing
- How to lead with credibility in your opening sentence
- Why shorter emails (3–4 sentences) often perform better

If you're rebuilding email sequences for 2026, this article outlines practical steps you can test immediately.

Best regards,
James
Co-Host, Outfoxed

Hunter Web Services, Inc.

subject: Re: Inquiry about Mercury Language and IAM integration
body:
Hi John,

Thank you for your interest in Aetheros! We're happy to provide more information about Mercury and its integration with our IAM service.

Mercury simplifies RBAC implementation through its declarative syntax and built-in security features. You can define roles and permissions directly within your API code using annotations and decorators. These definitions are then enforced by the Aetheros runtime, ensuring that only authorized users can access specific API endpoints or resources.

Mercury provides dedicated libraries for interacting with the Aetheros IAM service. These libraries offer functions for retrieving user roles, checking permissions, and managing authentication tokens. This tight integration allows for streamlined RBAC implementation within your Mercury code.

For code examples and detailed documentation, please visit our documentation portal at [link to documentation]. You'll find a comprehensive guide on using Mercury and Aetheros IAM for secure API development.

Please don't hesitate to contact us if you have any further questions.

Best regards,
Aetheros Support Team
